Excerpts from Spanda-KДҒrikДҒs The Divine Creative Pulsation Part 3

16 Aug 2020 | Shaivism Kashmir-Shaivism Book-Excerpts

Selected Commentary of Kб№ЈemarДҒja

EXPOSITION

When the yogi sees every object as an expression of the inner divine Self, then the delusion of diversity disappears and he has the bliss of unity consciousness. He finds Еҡiva both within and without.

Verse 11

Text of the verse 14 аӨӘаӨ°аӨҫаӨ®аҘғаӨӨаӨ°аӨёаӨҫаӨӘаӨҫаӨҜаӨёаҘҚаӨӨаӨёаҘҚаӨҜ аӨҜаӨғ аӨӘаҘҚаӨ°аӨӨаҘҚаӨҜаӨҜаҘӢаӨҰаҘҚаӨӯаӨөаӨғ аҘӨ аӨӨаҘҮаӨЁаӨҫаӨёаҘҚаӨөаӨӨаӨЁаҘҚаӨӨаҘҚаӨ°аӨӨаӨҫаӨ®аҘҮаӨӨаӨҝ аӨё аӨҡ аӨӨаӨЁаҘҚаӨ®аӨҫаӨӨаҘҚаӨ°аӨ—аҘӢаӨҡаӨ°аӨғ аҘҘаҘ§аҘӘаҘҘ

рқҳ—рқҳўрқҳіДҒрқҳ®б№ӣрқҳөрқҳўрқҳірқҳўрқҳҙДҒрқҳұДҒрқҳәрқҳўрқҳҙ рқҳөрқҳўрқҳҙрқҳәрқҳў рқҳәрқҳўбёҘ рқҳұрқҳірқҳўрқҳөрқҳәрқҳўрқҳәрқҳ°рқҳҘрқҳЈрқҳ©рқҳўрқҳ·рқҳўбёҘ рқҳӣрқҳҰрқҳҜДҒрқҳҙрқҳ·рқҳўрқҳөрқҳўрқҳҜрқҳөрқҳірқҳўрқҳөДҒрқҳ® рқҳҰрқҳөрқҳӘ рқҳҙрқҳў рқҳӨрқҳў рқҳөрқҳўрқҳҜрқҳ®ДҒрқҳөрқҳірқҳўрқҳЁрқҳ°рқҳӨрқҳўрқҳірқҳўбёҘ (рқҹ·рқҹә)

The rise, in the bound soul, of all sorts of ideas marks the disappearance of the bliss of supreme immortality. On account of this, he loses his independence. The appearance of the ideas has its sphere in sense-objects.

Selected Commentary of Kб№ЈemarДҒja

The rise in that bound soul of ideas whether pertaining to this world or the scriptures and of the knowledge of different objects associated with them leads to ruination. That marks the disappearance or subsidence of the bliss of supreme immortality i.e. of the flow of the bliss of the mass of consciousness. Though the state of supreme consciousness is present even when the ideas conveying the sense of different objects arise yet because it is not noticed, it appears to be absent. Hence it has been said that the bliss of supreme immortality disappears. By the rise of those ideas, he (the bound soul), loses his independence i.e. comes under their clutches. As has been said in Еҡiva-sЕ«tra, вҖҳLimited knowledge is the cause of bondageвҖқ. (I, 2)

Text of the 16th Verse

аӨёаҘҮаӨҜаӨӮ аӨ•аҘҚаӨ°аӨҝаӨҜаӨҫаӨӨаҘҚаӨ®аӨҝаӨ•аӨҫ аӨ¶аӨ•аҘҚаӨӨаӨҝаӨғ аӨ¶аӨҝаӨөаӨёаҘҚаӨҜ аӨӘаӨ¶аҘҒаӨөаӨ°аҘҚаӨӨаӨҝаӨЁаҘҖаҘӨ аӨ¬аӨЁаҘҚаӨ§аӨҜаӨҝаӨӨаҘҚаӨ°аҘҖ аӨёаҘҚаӨөаӨ®аӨҫаӨ°аҘҚаӨ—аӨёаҘҚаӨҘаӨҫ аӨңаҘҚаӨһаӨҫаӨӨаӨҫ аӨёаӨҝаӨҰаҘҚаӨ§аҘҚаӨҜаҘҒаӨӘаӨӘаӨҫаӨҰаӨҝаӨ•аӨҫ аҘҘ аҘ§аҘ¬ аҘҘ

рқҳҡрқҳҰрқҳәрқҳўб№ғ рқҳ¬рқҳірқҳӘрқҳәДҒрқҳөрқҳ®рқҳӘрқҳ¬ДҒ Еӣрқҳўрқҳ¬рқҳөрқҳӘбёҘ ЕӣрқҳӘрқҳ·рқҳўрқҳҙрқҳәрқҳў рқҳұрқҳўЕӣрқҳ¶рқҳ·рқҳўрқҳірқҳөрқҳӘрқҳҜД« рқҳүрқҳўрқҳҜрқҳҘрқҳ©рқҳўрқҳәрқҳӘрқҳөрқҳіД« рқҳҙрқҳ·рқҳўрқҳ®ДҒрқҳірқҳЁрқҳўрқҳҙрқҳөрқҳ©ДҒ рқҳ«ГұДҒрқҳөДҒ рқҳҙрқҳӘрқҳҘрқҳҘрқҳ©рқҳәрқҳ¶рқҳұрқҳўрқҳұДҒрқҳҘрқҳӘрқҳ¬ДҒ (рқҹ·рқҹј)

That afore-mentioned operative power of Еҡiva existing in the bound soul is a source of bondage; the same when realized as residing in him as the way of approach to oneвҖҷs own essential reality brings about success (i.e. the achievement of liberation).

Selected Commentary of Kб№ЈemarДҒja

IyamвҖ”This one, the operative power which is oneвҖҷs own real nature i.e. of the nature of consciousness identical with Еҡiva. She has been called вҖңthisвҖқ, because she manifests herself in the form of objectivity. She is the venerable Spanda principle. She is known as operative power (kriyДҒ Еӣakti), because she brings about the state of variety of the universe. As described before, she exists in Еҡiva who has assumed the role of a bound soul. She besprinkles this bound soul who is identified with prДҒб№Үa and puryб№Јб№ӯaka with a drop of I-consciousness wh makes him an agent or doer. Reduced to this state, he does not recognize his real nature which is veiled by her and gets involved in the misery of seizing and relinquishing. Thus she becomes a source of bondage. When the yogi recognizes this operative power as the Supreme power, (parДҒ Еӣakti), the means of approach to his real nature which is Еҡiva, or when he, in spite of the dispersion of definite and indefinite ideas, regards the entire objectivity as an aspect of his own Self which in its nature is Еҡiva, then also she (Еӣakti) brings about the same achievement.

EXPOSITION

On account of the play of MДҒyДҒ Еӣakti, the soul loses its pristine jГұДҒna and kriyДҒ Еӣakti and comes to have only limited knowledge and limited power of action. Secondly, he falls a prey to the veiled powers that arise from the multitude of words. In his present life, he acquires ideas through his experience of sense-objects, and education, but these ideas are not possible without words. So ultimately words come to acquire tremendous influence over his life.

The puryб№Јб№ӯaka plays the most important role. Man is a very complex being. He has not only a physical body but also a subtle one known as puryб№Јб№ӯaka which consists of the five tanmДҒtras or the subtle aspects of the gross physical objects and buddhi, manas and ahaб№ҒkДҒra. The impressions of our desires and thoughts are deposited in this puryб№Јб№ӯaka. When a man dies, it is only his physical body that is dissolved. The puryasИӣaka remains as the subtle vehicle of the soul after his death. As has already been said, it contains the residual traces of the desires, etc. of the previous life. The desires and ideas deposited in the puryб№Јб№ӯaka are not inert elements but tremendous psychic forces seeking expression. So in the next life, man gets a body suited for the expression of the desires, etc., deposited in the puryб№Јб№ӯaka and is born in an environment suited for that expression.

Verse 18, Exposition
